metadata.title: Retrieval trace bundle
metadata.subtitle: Bundle fixture stressing evidence provenance
metadata.description: A bundle shaped more like retrieval output than a hand-authored UI packet.
watchFor: Evidence entries should produce both support nodes and citation nodes.
Low-confidence answer phrases should survive as tokens instead of disappearing into prose.
Figures should attach through evidence links without manual packet relations.

query.text: Why do retrieval-heavy AI interfaces need something beyond card stacks?

answer.title: Retrieval surfaces need more than cards
answer.statement: When evidence, provenance, and uncertainty all need to remain co-visible, a simple stack of rigid cards starts flattening the semantics.
answer.body:
A retrieval-heavy interface has to keep citations, contradictions, and images in view without hiding the answer itself.

A solver-driven surface can negotiate those relationships directly instead of forcing everything through one reading column.

evidence.excerpt: Citation-bearing evidence needs to stay near the answer while still preserving enough separation to inspect source quality.
Images often belong to the evidence chain rather than to the answer text, but box stacks usually force them into detached side panels.

issues.text: If relation strength is weak, evidence clusters can still collapse into a confusing pile.
